data.title: Gorman-Rupp Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 misses by $0.02, revenue of $179M beats by $4.4M
data.description:
• Gorman-Rupp press release (NYSE:GRC): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $179M (+5.6% Y/Y) beats by $4.4M.
  • The Company’s backlog of orders was $224.4 million at June 30, 2025 compared to $206.0 million at December 31, 2024, and $224.4 million at June 30, 2024.

data.title: GrafTech International Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.16 misses by $0.06, revenue of $131.84M beats by $1.4M
data.description:
• GrafTech International press release (NYSE:EAF): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.16 misses by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $131.84M (-4.0% Y/Y) beats by $1.4M.
  • Sales volume of 28.6 thousand MT
  • Now project a 7-9% year-over-year decline in our cash costs per MT for 2025 on a full-year basis, exceeding our previous guidance range of a mid-single digit percentage point decline.

data.title: Puma slides after soft earnings, plans to raise prices in the U.S. to offset tariffs
data.description:
Puma SE (OTCPK:PMMAF) fell sharply on Friday after the German sportswear brand posted a weak FQ2 earnings report and warned of a profit loss for the full year. 

On a preliminary basis, Puma (OTCPK:PMMAF) sales for Q2 declined currency-adjusted by 2.0% to €1.94 billion. Currencies were noted to be a major headwind. The sales decline was driven by the key markets North America (-9.1%), Europe (-3.9%), and Greater China (-3.9%). While the sales in the rest of APAC also declined.

Amid ongoing volatile geopolitical and macroeconomic volatility, Puma (OTCPK:PMMAF) anticipates that both sector-wide and company-specific challenges will continue to significantly impact performance in 2025. Key factors include muted brand momentum, shifts in channel mix and quality, the impact of U.S. tariffs, and elevated inventory levels. 

Puma (OTCPK:PMMAF) now expects an annual EBIT amid the choppy backdrop. CEO Arthur Hoeld said the company needs to "course-correct."

"This year, 2025, will be a reset for Puma, and 2026 will be a transition year for us," he noted. "We do have tremendous potential with a brand that hasn't been unlocked yet, but a brand that also requires a reset and a new way forward."

Puma (OTCPK:PMMAF) plans to raise prices in the U.S. to offset its tariff impact.

Shares of Puma (OTCPK:PMMAF) were down 18% in Frankfurt trading. Adidas (OTCQX:ADDYY) shed 1.5%.

data.title: Central Pacific Financial GAAP EPS of $0.67 beats by $0.03, revenue of $72.8M beats by $1M
data.description:
• Central Pacific Financial press release (NYSE:CPF): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.67 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $72.8M (+13.7% Y/Y) beats by $1M.
  • Return on average assets of 1.00% and return on average equity of 13.04%.
  • The company recorded a provision for credit losses of $5.0 million in the second quarter of 2025, compared to a provision of $4.2 million in the previous quarter and a provision of $2.2 million in the year-ago quarter.
  • The allowance for credit losses, as a percentage of total loans, was 1.13% at June 30, 2025, compared to 1.13% at March 31, 2025, and 1.16% at June 30, 2024.
  • Efficiency ratio improved to 60.36%.
  • Net interest margin of 3.44% increased by 13 bps from 3.31% in the previous quarter.
  • Total risk-based capital and common equity tier 1 ratios of 15.8% and 12.6%, respectively.
  • The CPF Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.27 per share.

data.title: Congo aims to set cobalt price to encourage domestic processing
data.description:
The Democratic Republic of Congo is aiming to set a cobalt price that supports domestic processing as the government weighs its next steps following a ban on cobalt exports, Bloomberg reported on Friday, citing the chairman of the state mining company.

Congo, which accounts for about three-quarters of global cobalt supply, suspended shipments for four months on Feb. 22, before extending the ban by three months in June.

The decision came after prices slumped in recent years as output soared, particularly from two mines operated by China’s CMOC Group (OTCPK:CMCLF).

Though cobalt prices spiked initially, the impact was dampened by high global inventory levels. Analysts confirmed that inventories, particularly in China, still represent about 8–10 months of demand.

data.title: First Citizens BancShares Non-GAAP EPS of $44.78 beats by $5.69, revenue of $2.38B beats by $170M
data.description:
• First Citizens BancShares press release (NASDAQ:FCNCA): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $44.78 beats by $5.69.
  • Revenue of $2.38B (-3.3% Y/Y) beats by $170M.
  • Net interest margin ("NIM") was 3.26% in both the current and linked quarters as the favorable impact of a lower rate paid on interest-bearing deposits was offset by the unfavorable impacts of a higher average balance of interest-bearing deposits and borrowings, a higher rate paid on borrowings, and lower PAA. NIM, excluding PAA, was 3.14% compared to 3.12% in the linked quarter.
